title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: autosync R package implements WebSocket sync server Automerge CRDT documents. implements automerge-repo protocol, enabling R serve synchronization hub Automerge clients R, JavaScript, Rust, languages.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-server.R")  # Check package (R CMD check) devtools::check()  # Build documentation devtools::document()  # Install the package locally devtools::install()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Requires development versions packages: - automerge posit-dev/automerge-r - nanonext r-lib/nanonext@stream branch Install : pak::pak("shikokuchuo/autosync")

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Server (R/server.R): amsync_server() creates WebSocket server using nanonext’s http_server(). server maintains state environments : - documents - Loaded Automerge documents keyed document ID - sync_states - Per-client, per-document sync states (nested: sync_states[[client_id]][[doc_id]]) - connections - WebSocket connection objects keyed temp ID client ID - doc_peers - Document--peer mapping broadcasting Handlers (R/handlers.R): Message routing via handle_message() dispatches type-specific handlers: - handle_join - Protocol handshake, validates version “1”, authentication - handle_sync - Document synchronization using Automerge sync protocol - handle_ephemeral - Transient message forwarding (point--point broadcast) - broadcast_sync - Propagates changes peers subscribed document Auth (R/auth.R): Optional OAuth2 authentication via auth_config(). Validates Google OAuth2 tokens, supports email/domain allowlists custom validators. TLS mandatory auth enabled. Uses later::later() auth timeout enforcement. Client (R/client.R): amsync_fetch() implements client-side protocol fetching documents automerge-repo server. Storage (R/storage.R): Persistence layer using .automerge files configurable data directory.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Dual connection indexing: Pre-handshake connections keyed temp WebSocket ID (ws$id character); post-handshake, connection object also indexed client’s senderId join message. must cleaned disconnect. Storage ID semantics: NULL = auto-generate persistent ID, NA = ephemeral server (storage ID peer response), string = explicit storage ID. Environment-based state: server state uses environments (pass--reference), lists. intentional mutability.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Messages CBOR-encoded binary frames. Key message types: - join/peer - Connection handshake peer IDs metadata - request/sync - Document sync Automerge sync state data - ephemeral - Non-persisted messages real-time features - error/doc-unavailable - Error handling Document IDs Base58Check-encoded 16-byte random values. Peer IDs Base64-encoded.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: automerge - CRDT operations (am_create, am_sync_encode/decode, am_save/load) nanonext - WebSocket server async /O secretbase - CBOR encoding (cborenc/cbordec) Base58/Base64 later - Event loop integration (run_now async recv)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tests use port 0 (OS-assigned) default, retrieving actual URL via server$url. Test files cover server, client, handlers, storage, integration scenarios. Handler tests use mock WebSocket objects (create_mock_ws() create_test_state() test-handlers.R) test message handling without network /O Auth tests use local_mocked_bindings() mock Google token validation snapshot tests error messages Integration tests use skip_on_cran() network-dependent scenarios Cleanup: Tests use .exit() consistently tempfile() isolated storage directories

title: Automerge Sync Server for R
text: R sync server Automerge CRDT documents. Implements automerge-repo WebSocket protocol, enabling R serve synchronization hub Automerge clients R, JavaScript, Rust, languages.

title: Automerge Sync Server for R
text: Full automerge-repo protocol compatibility (interoperates sync.automerge.org) Secure connections via TLS (wss://) Persistent document storage Ephemeral message broadcasting Client functions fetching documents remote servers

title: Automerge Sync Server for R
code: pak::pak("shikokuchuo/autosync")

title: Automerge Sync Server for R
text: Create WebSocket sync server: TLS secure connections:
code: library(autosync)  server <- amsync_server() server$start() server  # Server runs non-blocking in the background  server$close() cert <- nanonext::write_cert() tls <- nanonext::tls_config(server = cert$server)  server <- amsync_server(tls = tls) server$start() server$url

title: Automerge Sync Server for R
text: Enable OIDC authentication restrict access. defaults point Google, OIDC-compliant provider (Microsoft Entra, Okta, Auth0, etc.) can used setting issuer argument. Set OIDC_CLIENT_ID OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables (e.g. .Renviron) OAuth credentials: Clients obtain token interactively, pass fetching:
code: cert <- nanonext::write_cert() tls <- nanonext::tls_config(server = cert$server)  server <- amsync_server(   tls = tls,  # TLS required when auth is enabled   auth = auth_config(allowed_domains = "posit.co") ) server$start() token <- amsync_token()  tlsclient <- nanonext::tls_config(client = cert$client) doc <- amsync_fetch(   server$url,   "35ei6ouA7nLhtjmf3d9xk1KKvtKv",   token = token,   tls = tlsclient )

title: Automerge Sync Server for R
text: share parameter controls documents announced clients requests allowed. accepts TRUE, FALSE, NA, function returning one per client document: Announce documents every client connects: Combine authentication use share function fine-grained access control. function receives client_id doc_id, can look authenticated email connection decide:
code: server <- amsync_server(share = TRUE) # Allow list of emails that can access documents allowed <- c("alice@example.com", "bob@example.com")  server <- amsync_server(   tls = tls,   auth = auth_config(),   share = function(client_id, doc_id) {     state <- attr(server, "sync")     conn <- state$connections[[client_id]]     if (conn$authenticated_email %in% allowed) NA else FALSE   } )

title: Automerge Sync Server for R
code: # Create a new document doc_id <- create_document(server)  # List all documents list_documents(server)  # Get a document doc <- get_document(server, doc_id)

code: # Fetch from public sync server doc <- amsync_fetch("wss://sync.automerge.org", "your-document-id")  # Inspect document structure str(doc)  # Or verbose fetch for debugging sync issues doc <- amsync_fetch("wss://sync.automerge.org", "your-document-id", verbose = TRUE)

what: Fetch a document from a sync server — amsync_fetch
title: Fetch a document from a sync server — amsync_fetch
text: Connects automerge-repo sync server retrieves document ID. useful debugging sync issues fetching documents remote servers like sync.automerge.org.

title: Fetch a document from a sync server — amsync_fetch
code: amsync_fetch(   url,   doc_id,   timeout = 5000L,   tls = NULL,   token = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fetch a document from a sync server — amsync_fetch
text: url WebSocket URL sync server (e.g., "ws://localhost:3030/" "wss://sync.automerge.org/"). Note: trailing slash may required. doc_id Document ID (base58check encoded string) timeout Timeout milliseconds receive operation. Default 5000. tls (optional) secure wss:// connections servers self-signed custom CA certificates, TLS configuration object created nanonext::tls_config(). token (optional) JWT (ID token) authenticated servers. Sent Bearer token Authorization header WebSocket upgrade request. verbose Logical, print debug messages. Default FALSE.

title: Fetch a document from a sync server — amsync_fetch
text: automerge document object containing fetched data.

title: Fetch a document from a sync server — amsync_fetch
text: function implements automerge-repo sync protocol: Connects via WebSocket Sends join message peer ID Receives peer response server Sends sync request specified document Receives applies sync messages complete Sync considered complete new messages arrive within timeout least one sync round.

title: Fetch a document from a sync server — amsync_fetch
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # Fetch from public sync server doc <- amsync_fetch("wss://sync.automerge.org", "4F63WJPDzbHkkfKa66h1Qrr1sC5U")  # Fetch from local server with debug output doc <- amsync_fetch(server$url, "myDocId", verbose = TRUE)  # Fetch from server with self-signed certificate cert <- nanonext::write_cert() tls <- nanonext::tls_config(client = cert$client) doc <- amsync_fetch(server$url, "myDocId", tls = tls)  # Fetch from authenticated server doc <- amsync_fetch(   "wss://secure.example.com",   "myDocId",   token = "eyJhbGciOi..." )  # Inspect the document automerge::am_keys(doc) }

what: Create an Automerge sync server — amsync_server
title: Create an Automerge sync server — amsync_server
text: Creates WebSocket server implements automerge-repo sync protocol, compatible JavaScript, Rust, Automerge clients.

title: Create an Automerge sync server — amsync_server
code: amsync_server(   port = 0L,   host = "127.0.0.1",   data_dir = ".automerge",   auto_create_docs = TRUE,   storage_id = NULL,   tls = NULL,   auth = NULL,   share = NA )

title: Create an Automerge sync server — amsync_server
text: port Port listen . Default 0 (binds random available port). actual URL retrieved via server$url. host Host address bind . Default "127.0.0.1" (localhost). data_dir Directory persistent document storage. Default ".automerge". auto_create_docs Logical, whether auto-create documents clients request unknown document IDs. Default TRUE. storage_id Optional storage ID server. NULL (default), generates new persistent identity. Set NA ephemeral server (persistence identity). tls (optional) secure wss:// connections, TLS configuration object created nanonext::tls_config(). auth Optional authentication configuration created auth_config(). provided, clients must include valid JWT (ID token) Bearer token Authorization header WebSocket upgrade request. Connections without valid credentials rejected immediately. Note: TLS required authentication enabled protect tokens. share Controls document sharing policy connected clients. unified parameter governs proactive document announcement (pushing documents clients) access control (allowing clients request documents). Accepts one : NA (default) — never announce allow requests. TRUE — announce documents clients allow requests. FALSE — never announce deny requests (sends doc-unavailable). function signature function(client_id, doc_id) returning TRUE (announce allow), NA (allow request ), FALSE (deny access). Called per client per document.

title: Create an Automerge sync server — amsync_server
text: amsync_server object inheriting 'nanoServer', $start() $close() methods.

title: Create an Automerge sync server — amsync_server
text: returned server inherits nanonext's nanoServer class provides $start() $close() methods non-blocking operation.

title: Create an Automerge sync server — amsync_server
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # Create and start a server server <- amsync_server() server$start()  # Server is now running in the background # ...do other work...  # Stop when done server$close()  # With TLS for secure connections cert <- nanonext::write_cert() tls <- nanonext::tls_config(server = cert$server) server <- amsync_server(tls = tls) server$start() server$url server$close()  # Server with OIDC authentication (requires TLS) cert <- nanonext::write_cert() tls <- nanonext::tls_config(server = cert$server) server <- amsync_server(   tls = tls,   auth = auth_config(     client_id = "123456789.apps.googleusercontent.com",     allowed_domains = "mycompany.com"   ) ) }

what: Obtain an OIDC token interactively — amsync_token
title: Obtain an OIDC token interactively — amsync_token
text: Performs OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow PKCE obtain JWT (ID token) OIDC provider. Opens system browser user authenticate, returns ID token use amsync_fetch().

title: Obtain an OIDC token interactively — amsync_token
code: amsync_token(   client_id = Sys.getenv("OIDC_CLIENT_ID"),   client_secret = Sys.getenv("OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET"),   issuer = oidc_issuer(),   scopes = "openid email",   redirect_uri = "http://localhost:5173",   timeout = 120 )

title: Obtain an OIDC token interactively — amsync_token
text: client_id OIDC client ID (application ID). Defaults OIDC_CLIENT_ID environment variable. client_secret OIDC client secret. Required "Web application" client types. needed "Desktop app" client types (use PKCE ). Defaults OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET environment variable. issuer OIDC issuer URL. Defaults OIDC_ISSUER environment variable, falling back Google ("https://accounts.google.com"). scopes Space-separated OAuth scopes request. Default "openid email". redirect_uri Local redirect URI OAuth callback. Default "http://localhost:5173". Must match redirect URI registered OIDC provider. timeout Seconds wait user complete authentication. Default 120.

title: Obtain an OIDC token interactively — amsync_token
text: JWT (ID token) character string.

title: Obtain an OIDC token interactively — amsync_token
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # Uses OIDC_CLIENT_ID and OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET env vars by default token <- amsync_token()  # Or supply credentials directly token <- amsync_token(   client_id = "YOUR_CLIENT_ID.apps.googleusercontent.com",   client_secret = "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET" )  # Use with amsync_fetch doc <- amsync_fetch(server$url, "myDocId", token = token, tls = tls) }

title: Create an authentication configuration — auth_config
text: Creates configuration object enabling OIDC JWT authentication autosync server. enabled, clients must include valid JWT (ID token) Bearer token Authorization header WebSocket upgrade request. Connections without valid credentials rejected immediately connection time.

title: Create an authentication configuration — auth_config
code: auth_config(   issuer = oidc_issuer(),   client_id = Sys.getenv("OIDC_CLIENT_ID"),   allowed_emails = NULL,   allowed_domains = NULL,   custom_validator = NULL )

title: Create an authentication configuration — auth_config
text: issuer OIDC issuer URL. used discover provider's public keys via .well-known/openid-configuration endpoint, validate iss claim JWTs. Defaults OIDC_ISSUER environment variable, falling back Google ("https://accounts.google.com"). client_id OIDC client ID (application ID). Validated aud claim JWTs. Defaults OIDC_CLIENT_ID environment variable. allowed_emails Character vector allowed email addresses. allowed_domains Character vector allowed email domains (e.g., "mycompany.com"). custom_validator Function(claims) returning TRUE/FALSE custom validation logic. Receives decoded JWT claims list.

title: Create an authentication configuration — auth_config
text: amsync_auth_config object.

title: Create an authentication configuration — auth_config
text: Works OIDC-compliant identity provider: Google, Microsoft Entra, Okta, Auth0, etc.

title: Create an authentication configuration — auth_config
code: # Google (default issuer) auth_config(   client_id = "123456789.apps.googleusercontent.com",   allowed_domains = "mycompany.com" ) #> $issuer #> [1] "https://accounts.google.com" #>  #> $client_id #> [1] "123456789.apps.googleusercontent.com" #>  #> $allowed_emails #> NULL #>  #> $allowed_domains #> [1] "mycompany.com" #>  #> $custom_validator #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "amsync_auth_config"  # Microsoft Entra auth_config(   issuer = "https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/v2.0",   client_id = "abcdef-1234-5678",   allowed_emails = "alice@mycompany.com" ) #> $issuer #> [1] "https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/v2.0" #>  #> $client_id #> [1] "abcdef-1234-5678" #>  #> $allowed_emails #> [1] "alice@mycompany.com" #>  #> $allowed_domains #> NULL #>  #> $custom_validator #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "amsync_auth_config"  # Custom validator auth_config(   issuer = "https://dev-123456.okta.com/oauth2/default",   client_id = "0oaXXXXXXXX",   custom_validator = function(claims) "editors" %in% claims$groups ) #> $issuer #> [1] "https://dev-123456.okta.com/oauth2/default" #>  #> $client_id #> [1] "0oaXXXXXXXX" #>  #> $allowed_emails #> NULL #>  #> $allowed_domains #> NULL #>  #> $custom_validator #> function (claims)  #> "editors" %in% claims$groups #> <environment: 0x55ebf6ae3d40> #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "amsync_auth_config"
